# VER-QUIC-CRT-0012 - QUIC Server-Role Certificate Continuation Verification

## Scope

This slice verifies only the permanent server-role continuation from peer ClientHello and local ServerHello through local EncryptedExtensions and local Certificate, plus Handshake packet-protection material availability. It does not prove CertificateVerify, server Finished, client-certificate authentication, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0114

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the narrow server-role local Certificate continuation behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully.
- The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the supported server-role Certificate continuation path, outbound local Certificate exposure, Handshake key-availability publication ordering, offset semantics, local leaf-certificate availability gating, transcript append behavior, and repeated or conflicting progression rejection.
- Run the prerequisite server-role floor tests that cover the supported ClientHello path, outbound ServerHello exposure, and the local EncryptedExtensions continuation.
- Inspect the bridge driver, transcript-progress owner, and key-schedule implementation to confirm the slice stays inside the main library seam, keeps runtime or host wiring unchanged, keeps commit unavailable, and does not add native TLS fallback.

## Expected Result

The server role emits one local Certificate after the supported ClientHello has been accepted, the local ServerHello has been published, local EncryptedExtensions has been published, Handshake keys are available, and the local leaf certificate DER validates as the supported single-leaf input; exposes those bytes through the existing Handshake crypto-output seam with correct offset semantics that place the Certificate immediately after local EncryptedExtensions; appends the local Certificate bytes at the correct transcript boundary immediately after local EncryptedExtensions; rejects premature or repeated local Certificate progression deterministically; and still leaves server-role commit and wider handshake support unavailable.

## Status

Passed on 2026-04-26; focused requirement-home tests prove the server-role continuation from the managed EncryptedExtensions floor into one local Certificate flight on the existing handshake seam, including offset semantics, local leaf-certificate gating, deterministic malformed and unsupported material rejection, repeated-progression rejection, and the continued absence of server-role commit. The prerequisite managed EncryptedExtensions floor remains separately traced by REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113 and its verification evidence.
